title: Rebuild GestureHotkeyApp from source and normalize repository for AI Orchestrator operation
bootstrap_instructions: Read ai-executor/AI_ORCHESTRATOR_RUNTIME.md because this task is executed via AI Orchestrator runtime JSON.
Read the target repository docs in this order: README.md, docs/codex-handoff-spec.md, docs/progress-log.md, docs/test-result-summary.md, docs/openmousegesture-mod-plan.md.
Search the repository for any other handoff, TASK, task, report, progress, Codex, Claude, or instruction documents before changing structure.
If a referenced file cannot be read, do not infer its contents. Record the unreadable file in runtime/report.json.
purpose: Restart GestureHotkeyApp / OpenMouseGesture development from the GitHub repository source, regenerate the missing exe/installer, identify the handoff instruction document, and bring the repository closer to the current AI Orchestrator operating structure without destructive cleanup.
background: The PC that originally produced prior build artifacts is no longer available, so any exe/installer that existed only on that PC is unrecoverable. GitHub does not contain the previous target/release build outputs. Therefore, do not spend time trying to recover old local artifacts. Treat the GitHub repository source as the only available base and rebuild the executable and NSIS installer from source. This repository was originally a non-GitHub working folder gathered into GitHub later, before the current AI Orchestrator operation was established. Its file layout may be inconsistent or cluttered. A previous runtime task failed before execution because git_sync pull_before_run failed with: fatal: Cannot fast-forward to multiple branches. Do not rewind or reuse the failed request. This is a new task. Known handoff candidate: docs/codex-handoff-spec.md. That document states that the current main work directory is source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/ and that legacy/GestureHotkeyApp-Wpf/ is reference-only. Current next priorities include build verification, Config.groups + Action.group_id UI behavior, group rename UX, action group move UI, export/import verification, and installer naming/final placement checks.
scope: In scope: repository inventory, handoff document discovery, current-operations normalization, project-level Markdown additions if missing, runtime JSON hygiene, fresh build from source, executable/installer regeneration, and a safe next-task recommendation. If small source/config fixes are required to restore the build baseline, they may be made within the declared target area. Out of scope: searching for or depending on old PC-only artifacts, destructive deletion, large file moves, history rewriting, broad redesign, unrelated refactoring, action label overlay re-enablement, release publication, installer execution, and edits to ai-common/ai-skills/ai-knowledge/ai-executor.
constraints: Do not perform destructive git operations such as reset --hard, clean -fd, force push, branch deletion, or unreviewed mass deletion.
Do not move or delete legacy/ or release/source folders during this task. If reorganization is needed, propose a plan first in runtime/report.json.
Do not run the installer unless a human explicitly permits it during this task.
Do not upload artifacts to GitHub Releases or any external service.
Do not commit generated target/ or installer outputs unless explicitly instructed later.
Do not re-enable action label overlay. It is intentionally disabled due to prior freezes and cursor/trajectory issues.
Do not make broad UI redesigns or unrelated refactors.
If local git synchronization is blocked, stop before code changes and report the exact local state and safe recovery options.
Keep output concise. Record details in runtime/report.json rather than long chat-style narration.
required_changes: Inspect local git state and identify whether the previous git pull failure still blocks execution.
Search for handoff/instruction documents. Confirm docs/codex-handoff-spec.md and list any additional candidates found.
Inventory the top-level repository structure and classify directories/files as current source, release/reference, docs, artifacts, legacy, runtime, or unknown.

If PROJECT.md is missing, create a concise PROJECT.md that states the current project purpose, active work directory, current source of truth, implemented state, intentionally disabled features, and next priorities.
If CHANGELOG.md is missing, create a concise CHANGELOG.md seeded from docs/progress-log.md at a summary level. Do not duplicate the full progress log.

If AGENTS.md is missing, create a minimal project-level AGENTS.md for execution agents that points to ai-executor rules, identifies the active work directory, and warns not to mix legacy WPF work into the current OpenMouseGesture line.

If docs/runtime-json-contract.md is missing, either create a small project-local note pointing to ai-orchestrator runtime JSON roles or report why it should remain centralized. Do not invent a conflicting schema.
Normalize runtime/progress.json and runtime/report.json for this new task_id so stale failed results from the previous task are not mistaken for current output.
If synchronization can be completed safely without destructive operations, synchronize the local checkout with the repository default branch.

From source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357, run a fresh build from source: npm install if needed, npm run build, and npm run tauri build or artifacts/run-tauri-build.cmd when the Visual Studio Build Tools environment is required.
Regenerate GestureHotkeyApp.exe and the NSIS installer from source. Do not rely on any pre-existing target/release outputs.
Verify whether GestureHotkeyApp.exe and the NSIS installer are generated at the expected paths after the fresh build.
Confirm whether the current codebase is ready for the next development step around grouped action UI behavior and action group movement.
Only if the build fails due to a small source/config issue inside the declared target area, apply a minimal fix and rerun the relevant build command.
validation: Record commands run and their pass/fail result.
List handoff/instruction documents found and identify the primary handoff document.
List repository normalization changes made, especially PROJECT.md, CHANGELOG.md, AGENTS.md, docs/runtime-json-contract.md, runtime/progress.json, and runtime/report.json.
Verify npm run tauri build or run-tauri-build.cmd result.
Check for source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/src-tauri/target/release/GestureHotkeyApp.exe after the fresh build.
Check for source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/GestureHotkeyApp_0.1.0_x64-setup.exe after the fresh build.

If source-v1.0.1/7-rate-OpenMouseGesture-b8f5357/src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/GestureHotkeyApp-setup.exe exists after the fresh build, report it; if not, explicitly report that it does not exist.
Separate verified results from unverified items.
completion_criteria: runtime/report.json is updated with success, failed, or blocked status for this task_id.
Primary handoff instruction document is identified.
Repository structure has either been minimally normalized or a safe normalization plan is reported.
Git synchronization status is clearly reported.
Fresh build from GitHub source has either generated exe/installer or the blocker is clearly identified.
Any changed files are listed explicitly.
Unverified items and next recommended task are listed explicitly.
No destructive git operation, release upload, installer execution, or overlay re-enablement was performed.
reporting_requirements: Write a concise runtime/report.json including: task_id, status, summary, primary handoff document, additional handoff candidates, repository structure inventory, normalization changes, git_sync result, commands run, files changed, validation results, regenerated artifact paths with file sizes/timestamps if available, unverified items, blockers, and recommended next task. Also update runtime/progress.json as appropriate during execution.
notes: Old PC-only build artifacts are unavailable and should not be treated as recoverable. Start from the GitHub source and rebuild. This task intentionally treats docs/codex-handoff-spec.md as the known handoff candidate, but the agent must still search for other handoff/instruction files. Do not delete clutter during this task. Prefer adding current-operation entrypoint files and reporting a safe cleanup plan.
